name: Reserve Bank of India
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **RBI CAPITAL-FLOW CURBS REMAIN OPERATIONAL — INR ₹94.74 (-0.5% 1D) STRENGTHENED FURTHER FROM MAY 5 RECORD ON BRENT COLLAPSE + DXY WEAKNESS; ₹95 TRIGGER DURABLY REVERSED.** RBI defensive toolkit (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction) held INR through ₹95.36 record May 5, contributed to pullback to ₹94.74 on May 7 morning. India 10Y G-Sec ~6.99% (-3-5bps from recent 7.05% high); softer crude easing inflation expectations + DXY weakness allowing FII debt return. About 50% crisis-linked, 50% RBI policy expectations. **The MOU framework is the structural relief catalyst:** if signed within 14 days, RBI may begin to wind down OMC special-credit-facility (Brent <$103 sustained 2-3 sessions trigger NOT yet activated but in-range — Brent $101.95 May 7 morning). FX reserves >$700B (~11mo import cover). Governor Malhotra's 'FX curbs won't remain forever' framing remains operative. **Decision-relevant signal:** if MOU signs and FII rotation reverses (with USD/INR ₹94, Brent $101, CAD pressure easing), RBI has structural room to ease policy rate from 5.25% — multi-quarter India equity tailwind setup. If Iran rejects MOU, INR weakens back toward ₹95-96 territory and RBI defensive escalation continues.
